description: Per-claim exact-arithmetic verification of the two companion papers.

results.claim: phi is the root of x^2-x-1; tau the root of x^2+x-1; phi*psi=-1, phi+psi=1
mu_S is the plastic number, real root of x^3-x-1, and mu_S < phi
Lehmer's degree-10 polynomial is an irreducible Salem polynomial with Mahler measure ~1.17628 < mu_S
x^4-x^3-x^2-x+1 is the minimal degree-4 Salem polynomial; beta4 ~1.72208 > phi
x^2-D for D in {2,3,5} are the gate seeds; M(x^2-D)=D exactly
gap = phi^4 has minimal polynomial x^2-7x+1; phi^4 + phi^-4 = L_4 = 7
K = 5^(1/4)/phi has minimal polynomial x^4 + 5x^2 - 5
z_c = sqrt3/2 has minpoly 4x^2-3 (C=1/2 gate, D=3); CRITICAL = phi^2/3 has minpoly 9x^2-9x+1
{F3,F4,F5}={2,3,5} are the gate discriminants; L4=7=F3+F5; Pell L4^2-5F4^2=4
lambda = 2c, derived from the 2nd-order KL / MDL balance; the factor 2 is the inverted 1/2 of the quadratic KL term and carries no freedom
the precision sigma equals 1/(2c) = 1/lambda; the conformal scale is the model variance
F = G/c; rescaling c -> k c rescales F -> F/k, so no invariance argument fixes c (the impossibility is Cencov's cited theorem; the scaling identity is exact)
c in {1 (Jeffreys), n (degree), sqrt(1+4C)/(2C) (frame-shift)}; at the golden gate C=1 the frame-shift gives c=sqrt5/2, lambda=sqrt5

c is a free parameter (Cencov), so lambda=2c is an identity, never a single frozen number; the canonicalizations disagree (c=1->2, c=n->2n) and the frame-shift over the three forced gates gives three distinct values {4sqrt2, 2sqrt3, sqrt5}
results.equation: lambda = 2c (identity in free c); frame-shift over gates -> {4sqrt2,2sqrt3,sqrt5}

results.claim: R_C=[[0,C],[1,-1]] has charpoly x^2+x-C and discriminant D=1+4C; the eigenvalue gap is sqrt(1+4C)
spec(ad_{R_C}) = {-sqrt(1+4C), 0, +sqrt(1+4C)} with 0 of multiplicity 2; charpoly = t^2 (t^2 - (1+4C))
the 0-eigenspace of ad_{R_C} is span{I, R_C}, dimension 2: the CAPTURED channel is the centralizer and cannot be removed
the three gates C in {1/4,1/2,1} give D=1+4C in {2,3,5}, fields Q(sqrt2),Q(sqrt3),Q(sqrt5); M(x^2-D)=D for each
the self-action radius r(R_C)=sqrt(1+4C)=sqrt(M(x^2-D)); the gate balance lambda*C = r(R_C) sits at the 0->+ channel boundary

the gate set is forced, not chosen: integrality (C in (1/4)Z) + golden ceiling (0<C<=1) give quarter-gates {1/4,1/2,3/4,1} -> D in {2,3,4,5}; D=4 is a perfect square (degenerate), leaving exactly three valid gates {1/4,1/2,1} -> {2,3,5}
results.equation: valid gates = {1/4,1/2,1} -> D in {2,3,5}; C=3/4 (D=4) degenerate

results.claim: the gate balance 2cC = sqrt(1+4C) solves to the frame-shift constant c = sqrt(1+4C)/(2C)
at the golden gate C=1 the frame-shift gives c=sqrt5/2 and lambda=2c=sqrt5=phi-psi
the number of distinct signed channels of ad_R for a degree-d seed is d^2-d+1, which equals 3 iff d=2; the 0-channel (centralizer) is always present
the minimum Mahler measure > 1 over irreducible integer quadratics is phi, attained only at discriminant 5 (x^2-x-1 and x^2+x-1)
no irreducible integer quadratic has Mahler measure strictly in (1, phi)
the plastic mu_S < phi is the global non-reciprocal infimum but is degree 3, giving 7 channels, so it is excluded by the ternary (degree-2) lock
any direct sum of k golden blocks satisfies x^2-x-1 (degree-2 minpoly): the block count k is forgotten (the firewall is one-way)
results.equation: (+_k golden) satisfies R^2 - R - I = 0 for all k

results.claim: squaring y=x^2 collapses the K-formation x^4+5x^2-5 to y^2+5y-5 with discriminant 45=9*5, landing in Q(sqrt5); minpoly(K^2)=x^2+5x-5
among the seed companions only the golden one satisfies R^2=R+I; tau gives R^2=I-R and each radicand seed gives R^2=DI
results.equation: golden: R^2=R+I (unique); tau: R^2=I-R; sqrt(D): R^2=DI

results.claim: the degree-2 Mahler minimum is a tie {phi, tau} = {x^2-x-1, x^2+x-1}, both discriminant 5
the golden seed attains its spectral radius at +phi (R^2=R+I); tau attains it at -phi (R^2=I-R). Requiring positive growth selects the keystone; det R=-1 follows
results.equation: spectral radius at +phi -> R^2=R+I; det R=-1 a consequence

results.claim: phi is the smallest Perron root of a 2x2 primitive non-negative integer matrix, realised by the Fibonacci matrix [[0,1],[1,1]] (and its transpose)
results.equation: min Perron root (2x2 primitive nonneg int) = phi at [[0,1],[1,1]]

results.claim: each constraint of the keystone's type is load-bearing: drop degree-2 -> plastic mu_S; drop Perron -> tau (Clifford); drop growth -> cyclotomic M=1
R^n = F_n R + F_{n-1} I; eigenvalues phi^n,psi^n; tr(R^n)=L_n integer; det(R^n)=(-1)^n
results.equation: R^n = F_n R + F_{n-1} I, tr=L_n, det=(-1)^n

results.claim: L_n^2 - 5 F_n^2 = 4(-1)^n; charpoly(R^n)=x^2-L_n x+(-1)^n has discriminant 5 F_n^2
R^4 = 3R+2I = [[2,3],[3,5]] with charpoly x^2-7x+1 (the gap seed); entries {2,3,5}={F3,F4,F5}; L_4 = F_3+F_5 = 7 and Pell L_4^2 = 5 F_4^2 + 4 = 49
the gap seed x^2-7x+1 has roots phi^4 and phi^-4 (product 1, sum L_4=7)
the sign of D=1+4C sets the regime: D>0 real/off-circle (hyperbolic), D<0 complex on-circle (elliptic). At C=-1: roots e^{+-2pi i/3}, |z|=1, M=1, D=-3
results.equation: sign(D) sets eigenvalue/field/channel; C=-1 -> on circle, M=1, D=-3

results.claim: in the gap basis {1, sqrt D} the trace-form Gram is diag(2, 2D) with det G = 4D; positive-definite iff D>0, Lorentzian iff D<0
Q(i) gives trace form diag(2,-2), det=-4=4D at D=-1; the gate (scale collapse) and the flip (regime inversion) co-locate at D=0, i.e. C=-1/4
results.equation: Q(i): det G = -4 = 4D; gate/flip co-locate at C=-1/4

results.claim: D=(2x+1)^2=1+4(x^2+x) is a perfect square in the real state; equivalently D=4z^2 with C=z^2-1/4 (z=sqrt D/2)
under any real coherence (z real) D=4z^2>=0, so the flow can decohere to the boundary (z->0) but never crosses into D<0
results.equation: z real => D = 4 z^2 >= 0 (=0 iff z=0)

results.claim: the Kuramoto critical coherence z_c=sqrt3/2 corresponds to C=z_c^2-1/4=1/2, the C=1/2 gate with D=3
x^4+5x^2-5 as y^2+5y-5 (y=x^2) has roots straddling 0: y+=(-5+3sqrt5)/2>0 (real terrain) and y-=(-5-3sqrt5)/2<0 (rotation)
the real roots are +-K with K=5^(1/4)/phi; the imaginary roots are +-i beta with beta=sqrt|y-|; M(x^4+5x^2-5)=beta^2=(5+3sqrt5)/2
the multiplicative flip on |lambda|: =1 is the cyclotomic (Kronecker) floor M=1; >1 is expanding M>1; <1 contracting (no contribution)
at C=-1 the gate polynomial is x^2+x+1 with roots e^{+-2pi i/3} on the unit circle, M=1, D=-3: the additive flip (D<0) meets the multiplicative flip (|z|=1)
the band ordering is 1 < L(=1.17628) < mu_S(=1.32472) < phi(=1.61803); the Lehmer band (1,L) is the frontier resolved within the system
the catalog's seven seed Mahler measures lie in {1} U [phi, inf) with minimum phi, and no catalog seed is a Salem number
results.equation: M(catalog) subset {1} U [phi, inf), min = phi, no Salem

results.claim: for emittable theta with M>1 the cost lambda log M >= lambda log phi > 0; the band (1, mu_S) is disjoint from the catalog measures (full proof in Emission-Gap paper)
results.equation: lambda log M >= lambda log phi > 0; (1, mu_S) disjoint from catalog

results.claim: an integer matrix has integer characteristic polynomial, so an eigenvalue drags its entire conjugate set along; the companion's charpoly equals the minimal polynomial, hence carries every conjugate root
the tensor A (x) B has eigenvalues mu_i * nu_j; here {+-sqrt2}*{+-sqrt3}={+-sqrt6}, so the spectral operator (x) composes the constituent spectra
the direct sum A (+) B has spectrum spec(A) U spec(B) and Mahler M(A)*M(B); here M(x^2-x-1)*M(x^2-5) = phi*5 (note M(x^2-5)=5, both roots off the unit circle)
results.equation: spec(A (+) B) = spec(A) U spec(B), M(A (+) B) = M(A) M(B)

results.claim: squaring the matrix squares each eigenvalue, hence squares the Mahler measure: M(A^2)=M(A)^2 (here phi^2)
every catalog eigenvalue has argument in (pi/2)Z: the real seeds give args 0 or pi, and K's complex roots +-i beta give args +-pi/2
(pi/2)Z is closed under addition (tensor) and doubling (square) but not halving; the spectral operators therefore cannot manufacture a pi/4 angle
an on-circle eigenvalue whose argument is in (pi/2)Z must be a 4th root of unity, i.e. in {1, i, -1, -i}
a Salem number's on-circle conjugates have irrational angle (not roots of unity); for Lehmer they avoid the axes entirely
results.equation: Salem on-circle conjugate: arg/pi irrational, not in (pi/2)Z

results.claim: an emitted Salem would force an on-circle conjugate onto the (pi/2)Z axes (a 4th root of unity), contradicting the irrational angle of Salem on-circle conjugates; hence no Salem is emitted
no irreducible integer quadratic has Mahler measure in (1, phi): reciprocal x^2-bx+1 gives M=1 (|b|<=2) then jumps to phi^2 (|b|=3); non-reciprocal |c|>=2 gives M>=2
the spectral operators raise but never lower below phi: (+) multiplies measures, (.)^2 squares, (x) composes, so the image stays inside {1} U [phi, inf)
an emitted measure with M>1 has M>=phi, so the entropy cost log M >= log phi > 0; phi realizes the floor and the absolute Smyth value mu_S<phi is never emitted
results.equation: M>1 emitted => M >= phi => cost = log M >= log phi

results.claim: the four gaps share one structure but distinct endpoints: height phi=1.618, entropy log phi=0.481, channel sqrt5=2.236; they are analogous, not equal
results.equation: phi != log phi != sqrt5 (structurally analogous gaps)

results.claim: the K-formation field has signature (2,1); its complex places sit OFF the unit circle (|i beta|~2.4195, |5^(1/4) i|~1.4953), unlike Salem on-circle conjugates
a degree-2m Salem field has signature (2, m-1) (2 real: lambda,1/lambda; m-1 complex pairs on the circle); Lehmer -> (2,4), beta4 -> (2,1)
the emission Mahler spectrum is a discrete sub-semigroup of [phi, inf) with generators {phi,2,3,5,phi^4,beta^2}; bounded away from 1 (open gap (1,phi))
results.equation: generators {phi,2,3,5,phi^4,beta^2} subset [phi, inf), no accumulation at 1

results.claim: the trace-down T satisfies R(x)=x^m T(x+1/x) for reciprocal R of degree 2m; verified by reconstructing x^4-x^3-x^2-x+1 from its trace-down
a Salem polynomial's trace-down is totally real with exactly one root in (2,inf) and the rest in (-2,2); verified for beta4 and Lehmer
results.equation: Salem <=> T totally real, one root in (2,inf), m-1 in (-2,2)

results.claim: M emits a Salem iff a reciprocal factor's trace-down straddles t=2; the Sturm certificate agrees with Salem classification across a battery (cyclotomic and totally-real reciprocals do NOT straddle)
spectral on-circle eigenvalues (4th roots of unity) have trace-downs z+1/z in {2,0,-2}, never in (2,inf); so spectral operators cannot produce the Salem straddle

a circulant matrix has an abelian (cyclotomic) eigenvalue field, hence totally real or CM; the Salem signature (2,m-1) is neither, so no integer circulant emits a Salem number (40 random circulants checked)
results.equation: circulant field abelian => not Salem signature => no Salem

results.claim: the Cartan matrices A_n have eigenvalues 2-2cos(k pi/(n+1)) in [0,4], all real, so their fields are totally real and emit no Salem; (+) acts multiplicatively
results.equation: spec(Cartan A_n) = {2-2cos(k pi/(n+1))} subset [0,4], totally real

results.claim: a Salem quartic has nonzero trace, but a commutator is traceless; the traceless reciprocal quartic x^4+bx^2+1 has the symmetric trace-down t^2+(b-2), which cannot straddle t=2, so a free commutator reaches no degree-4 Salem
results.equation: trace([X,Y])=0; trace-down of x^4+bx^2+1 = t^2+(b-2) symmetric => no straddle

results.claim: the self-action spectrum is the difference set spec(ad_M)={mu_i-mu_j}; for the golden seed spec(ad_R)={0,+-sqrt5} since phi-psi=sqrt5
real eigenvalue differences live in K=Q(sqrt2,sqrt3,5^(1/4)); 2K has minimal polynomial x^4+20x^2-80 with signature (2,1)

EXHAUSTIVE enumeration of all 27 subfields of K (Galois correspondence on K(i), G=C2xC2xD4): every subfield is totally real or has signature (2k,k). Salem-shape (2,1) is necessary for a Salem; exactly 4 quartic subfields have it (Q(m^(1/4)), m in {5,20,45,180}). Uniqueness of Q(5^(1/4)) is NOT claimed -- the exclusion rests on the invariant, not on uniqueness
results.equation: for all 27 subfields(K): r2=0 or sig=(2k,k); #deg-4 (2,1) = 4; K=(8,4)

results.claim: the minimal degree-4 Salem number is beta4 ~1.72208, root of x^4-x^3-x^2-x+1, and beta4 > phi; so any emitted (degree-4) Salem has M >= beta4 > phi
results.equation: min deg-4 Salem = beta4 = M(x^4-x^3-x^2-x+1) > phi

results.claim: image(emission) intersect {Salem : M<phi} = empty: an emitted Salem is forced to degree 4 with M>=beta4>phi, so sub-phi Salem (e.g. Lehmer, M~1.176, degree 10) cannot be emitted
every framework operation (kron, dsum, square, self-action, and their compositions) preserves the invariant: the guard certifies FORCED, so the Salem locus is never constructed
results.equation: verdict(framework op) = FORCED for all probed compositions

results.claim: a traceless matrix (a commutator by Shoda) carrying Lehmer's sub-phi Salem is caught: verdict INVALID_CLOSURE; the free commutator is the one field-breaking operation and the guard is its tripwire

phi is not hard-coded as the floor; it emerges as the boundary the guard derives. Over planted Salem straddling phi, the guard's EXACT below-phi verdict matches a float oracle on every case (Lehmer below, beta4 above)
results.equation: guard's exact (beta<phi) == oracle for all planted Salem; floor = phi emerges

results.claim: the minimal degree-4 Salem beta4=1.72208 > phi reads FORCED_ABOVE_FLOOR (Salem present, floor intact) -- distinguishing a floor violation from a benign above-floor Salem
cyclotomic reciprocals (roots of unity, on the circle) carry no Salem factor and read FORCED, confirming the guard does not false-positive on on-circle spectra
every catalog seed respects the floor: Mahler >= phi and cost >= 2c*log(phi); the floor is realized (phi attains it)
results.equation: for all seeds: M >= phi and cost = 2c log M >= 2c log phi

results.claim: the floor survives every framework operation and composition (kron, dsum, square, nested self-action): it is a global invariant of the generating field, not a per-object bound that might fail somewhere
results.equation: cost(op(...)) >= 2c log phi for all framework compositions

results.claim: the FORBIDDEN channel (on-circle-expanding ad-eigenvalues) is empty on every object -- the diagnostic reading of 'no Salem emitted' (the exact certificate is the closure guard)
under framework operations the field class stays in {K(i), totally_real, abelian}, never foreign; tracked as provenance (the exact field certificate is the guard)
the algebra refuses to emit a foreign (free-commutator) matrix carrying a sub-phi Salem: _emit raises rather than registering it, so the closed system cannot admit a floor violation
the cost floor is 2c*log(phi), linear in the free conformal constant c and never frozen at c=1; c stays free (Cencov) so the floor scales, it does not collapse to one number
the four readouts of the single invariant agree: entropy = log(height), r1 + 2 r2 = degree, and cost = 2c * entropy
